description: Stations, ships, and strange frontiers where technology shapes every horizon.
locations: Space Station
Metal corridors hum with recycled air and the soft glow of status panels. Outside the viewport, stars drift in silence that never ends.
Starship
The bridge stretches wide beneath a canopy of stars. Engines thrum through the deck plates, and every corridor leads somewhere new.
Moon Base
Low ceilings and filtered light beneath a sky that never changes. Footprints in the dust outside last forever, and the Earth hangs like a blue marble.
Mars Colony
Red dust coats every surface outside the pressurized domes. The sky is a thin pink, and the silence between buildings is alien and vast.
Cyber City
Neon signs flicker in a dozen languages above crowded streets. Data streams flow like rivers overhead, and the city never sleeps.
Orbital Lab
Weightlessness makes everything float, including the experiments. The view through the porthole rotates slowly between ocean, cloud, and starfield.
Wormhole
Space folds and light bends in spirals that make the eyes water. The passage between here and there takes a heartbeat and feels like forever.
Quantum Realm
Scale collapses. Particles shimmer in and out of existence, and the rules of the world above simply stop applying.
Colony Ship
Generations have lived and died aboard this vessel, crossing the dark between stars. The destination is a promise no one alive remembers making.
Cryovault
Rows of frosted pods line a corridor kept just above absolute zero. The sleepers inside dream slowly, if they dream at all.
Spaceport
Ships of every shape crowd the landing pads, and the air smells of fuel and foreign atmospheres. Travelers from a dozen worlds pass without a glance.
Abandoned Outpost
Dust coats the consoles and the lights flicker on emergency power. The last log entry ended mid-sentence. Something made the crew leave fast.
Cargo Bay
Crates stamped with colony codes stack floor to ceiling. The bay doors open onto vacuum, and the magnetic clamps hum with quiet tension.
Control Room
Screens line every wall, casting blue light across intent faces. Every switch matters, and the readouts change faster than anyone can track.
Terraformed World
The sky is almost the right color, and the trees grow in patterns that feel just slightly off. This world was shaped by hand, and it remembers.
Virtual Reality
The world renders around you in perfect detail, but the edges shimmer if you look too long. Nothing here can hurt you. Probably.
Junkyard
Scrapped ships and broken machines pile in rusting mountains under an open sky. Parts from a hundred worlds lie scattered, waiting to be useful again.
Quarantine Zone
Warning lights pulse along sealed corridors. The air is filtered twice, and the hazmat suits hanging by the door are not optional.
Space Elevator
A cable stretches from the ground to the sky and beyond, impossibly thin and impossibly strong. The car climbs in silence as the horizon curves.
